body.title: P1 fix loop: step-debug pe_chain one engram at a time, fix first wrong direction, repeat until clean solution
body.result: P1 fixed: ENGRAM basket isolation (fresh {} per MCPCALL) + pre-flight false-fail (exit code primary signal). Shipped in d7499f50.
body.description:
Use pe_chain_debugger.start(ticket_id, breakpoint='PLAN') then step_next() one step at a time. At the first basket state heading wrong (wrong file proposed, spurious HIGH-inertia flag, empty plan), fix the root cause in the code, step one more to verify, repeat until HYPOTHESIZE produces a correct plan with correct files for ticket scope. CC drives the loop — Igor does not need a full ticket cycle.

CRITICAL RULE: Do NOT read pe_chain.py source to reason about the bug. That approach has been tried and does not work. The only valid debug method is: run the engram debugger, watch basket state at each step, halt on first wrong state, fix that step's code.

All steps run in a single Python process (sessions are in-process only — step_next() across subprocesses loses state). Use timeout 300s minimum for LLM steps.

Greedy ticket selection still applies in parallel. Trip condition: pe_chain runs ENTRY→CLOSE for a simple S-size ticket without proposing HIGH-inertia edits outside ticket scope.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py, wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain_debugger.py
**Design rules:** docs-in-code (update pe_chain docstring with fix when found)
**Scope boundary:** IN — find and fix the specific step where basket first goes wrong. OUT — refactoring pe_chain, changing engram sequence.
**Test plan:** debugger runs T-no-sqlite-enforcement ENTRY→HYPOTHESIZE with correct files in basket, no HIGH-inertia proposals.
